ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "wban_no" INTEGER /* WBAN number of the weather station */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the weather station */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the weather station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the weather station */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the weather station */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the weather station was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the weather station */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "datasets" (
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station collecting the data */,
  "utc_date" TEXT /* UTC date of the data collection */,
  "utc_time" TEXT /* UTC time of the data collection */,
  "lst_date" TEXT /* Local standard time date of the data collection */,
  "lst_time" TEXT /* Local standard time of the data collection */,
  "crx_vn" REAL /* CRX version number */,
  "collection_method" TEXT /* Method used to collect the data */,
  "data_quality_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating the quality of the data */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  "data_collection_interval" TEXT /* Interval at which data is collected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_datasets_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "temperature_data" (
  "temperature_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each temperature data point */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the temperature data belongs to */,
  "t_calc" REAL /* Calculated temperature */,
  "t_hr_avg" REAL /* Hourly average temperature */,
  "t_max" REAL /* Maximum temperature */,
  "t_min" REAL /* Minimum temperature */,
  "t_avg" REAL /* Average temperature over the collection period */,
  "t_std_dev" REAL /* Standard deviation of temperature */,
  "t_quality_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating the quality of the temperature data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("temperature_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_temperature_data_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "solar_radiation_data" (
  "solar_radiation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each solar radiation data point */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the solar radiation data belongs to */,
  "solarad" REAL /* Solar radiation value */,
  "solarad_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the solar radiation value is valid */,
  "solarad_max" REAL /* Maximum solar radiation value */,
  "solarad_max_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the maximum solar radiation value is valid */,
  "solarad_min" REAL /* Minimum solar radiation value */,
  "solarad_min_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the minimum solar radiation value is valid */,
  "solarad_avg" REAL /* Average solar radiation over the collection period */,
  "solarad_std_dev" REAL /* Standard deviation of solar radiation */,
  "solarad_quality_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating the quality of the solar radiation data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("solar_radiation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_solar_radiation_data_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "surface_temperature_data" (
  "surface_temperature_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each surface temperature data point */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the surface temperature data belongs to */,
  "sur_temp" REAL /* Surface temperature value */,
  "sur_temp_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the surface temperature value is valid */,
  "sur_temp_max" REAL /* Maximum surface temperature value */,
  "sur_temp_max_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the maximum surface temperature value is valid */,
  "sur_temp_min" REAL /* Minimum surface temperature value */,
  "sur_temp_min_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the minimum surface temperature value is valid */,
  "sur_temp_type" TEXT /* Type of surface temperature (e.g., R, S) */,
  "sur_temp_avg" REAL /* Average surface temperature over the collection period */,
  "sur_temp_std_dev" REAL /* Standard deviation of surface temperature */,
  "sur_temp_quality_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating the quality of the surface temperature data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("surface_temperature_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_surface_temperature_data_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "soil_moisture_data" (
  "soil_moisture_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each soil moisture data point */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the soil moisture data belongs to */,
  "soil_moisture_5" REAL /* Soil moisture value at 5 cm depth */,
  "soil_moisture_10" REAL /* Soil moisture value at 10 cm depth */,
  "soil_moisture_20" REAL /* Soil moisture value at 20 cm depth */,
  "soil_moisture_50" REAL /* Soil moisture value at 50 cm depth */,
  "soil_moisture_100" REAL /* Soil moisture value at 100 cm depth */,
  "soil_moisture_avg_5" REAL /* Average soil moisture at 5 cm depth */,
  "soil_moisture_avg_10" REAL /* Average soil moisture at 10 cm depth */,
  "soil_moisture_avg_20" REAL /* Average soil moisture at 20 cm depth */,
  "soil_moisture_avg_50" REAL /* Average soil moisture at 50 cm depth */,
  "soil_moisture_avg_100" REAL /* Average soil moisture at 100 cm depth */,
  "soil_moisture_quality_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating the quality of the soil moisture data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("soil_moisture_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_soil_moisture_data_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "soil_temperature_data" (
  "soil_temperature_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each soil temperature data point */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the soil temperature data belongs to */,
  "soil_temp_5" REAL /* Soil temperature value at 5 cm depth */,
  "soil_temp_10" REAL /* Soil temperature value at 10 cm depth */,
  "soil_temp_20" REAL /* Soil temperature value at 20 cm depth */,
  "soil_temp_50" REAL /* Soil temperature value at 50 cm depth */,
  "soil_temp_100" REAL /* Soil temperature value at 100 cm depth */,
  "soil_temp_avg_5" REAL /* Average soil temperature at 5 cm depth */,
  "soil_temp_avg_10" REAL /* Average soil temperature at 10 cm depth */,
  "soil_temp_avg_20" REAL /* Average soil temperature at 20 cm depth */,
  "soil_temp_avg_50" REAL /* Average soil temperature at 50 cm depth */,
  "soil_temp_avg_100" REAL /* Average soil temperature at 100 cm depth */,
  "soil_temp_quality_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating the quality of the soil temperature data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("soil_temperature_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_soil_temperature_data_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "humidity_data" (
  "humidity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each humidity data point */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the humidity data belongs to */,
  "rh_hr_avg" REAL /* Hourly average relative humidity */,
  "rh_hr_avg_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the hourly average relative humidity value is valid */,
  "rh_avg" REAL /* Average relative humidity over the collection period */,
  "rh_std_dev" REAL /* Standard deviation of relative humidity */,
  "rh_quality_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating the quality of the humidity data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("humidity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_humidity_data_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or team the user belongs to */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the dataset */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the dataset was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the user accessed the dataset */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to access the dataset */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the user session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dataset_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset version */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the dataset */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of changes made in this version */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created this version */,
  "version_status" TEXT /* Status of the version (e.g., draft, published) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dataset_versions_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dataset_versions_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "metadata" (
  "metadata_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metadata entry */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset */,
  "metadata_key" TEXT /* Key of the metadata (e.g., sensor type, calibration date) */,
  "metadata_value" TEXT /* Value of the metadata */,
  "metadata_type" TEXT /* Type of metadata (e.g., sensor, calibration, environment) */,
  "metadata_description" TEXT /* Description of the metadata */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the metadata was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metadata_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_metadata_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)
